summary: The US and Israel signed a framework agreement with Lebanon requiring IDF withdrawal from southern Lebanon conditional on Hezbollah's disarmament, while CENTCOM conducted strikes on Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar in Hormozgan Province following Iran's attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran launched two drones at Bahrain, one intercepted and one falling without damage, and an oil tanker was hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz. Hezbollah's deputy secretary-general Naim Qassem rejected the Lebanon-Israel framework agreement as void and demanded its replacement with the US-Iran memorandum of understanding.
key_points: Israel and Lebanon signed a US-mediated framework agreement in Washington requiring IDF withdrawal from southern Lebanon conditional on Hezbollah disarmament, with a pilot in two areas where the Lebanese army will assume security responsibility. (Source: @yediotnews25/131976)

CENTCOM confirmed US aircraft struck Iranian missile and drone storage locations and coastal radar sites in Hormozgan Province on June 26 in response to Iran's one-way attack drone strike on the Singapore-flagged M/V Ever Lovely in the Strait of Hormuz on June 25. (Source: @shin_persian/7223)

Iran launched two drones at Bahrain; one was intercepted from the ground and one fell without causing damage, while Bahrain accused Iran of a drone attack targeting the island kingdom. (Source: @New_security8200/202119)

UKMTO reported an oil tanker was hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, causing damage to the vessel's bridge, with all crew reported safe and no environmental damage. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4423)
Hezbollah deputy secretary-general Naim Qassem declared the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement void and demanded it be replaced with the US-Iran memorandum of understanding. (Source: @pkpoi/142995)
IDF and Shin Bet confirmed the killing of Walid al-Nehé, deputy commander of the Nukhba company who commanded the October 7 infiltration into Israel, in Gaza. (Source: @almogboker78/17608)
Israeli drone strike targeted Al-Nabatiya in southern Lebanon, injuring at least one Lebanese civilian as ceasefire violations persist despite the framework agreement. (Source: @presstv/195884)

Iran's IRGC issued a statement asserting that the CENTCOM strikes on Sirik, Hormozgan Province, will not go unanswered and a swift decisive response will be delivered at a time and place of their choosing. (Source: @shin_persian/7227)

sections.executive_summary: The US and Israel signed a framework agreement with Lebanon requiring IDF withdrawal from southern Lebanon conditional on Hezbollah's disarmament, while CENTCOM conducted strikes on Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar in Hormozgan Province following Iran's attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran launched two drones at Bahrain, one intercepted and one falling without damage, and an oil tanker was hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz. Hezbollah's deputy secretary-general Naim Qassem rejected the Lebanon-Israel framework agreement as void and demanded its replacement with the US-Iran memorandum of understanding.

points: CENTCOM confirmed US aircraft struck Iranian missile and drone storage locations and coastal radar sites in Hormozgan Province on June 26, in response to Iran's one-way attack drone strike on the Singapore-flagged M/V Ever Lovely in the Strait of Hormuz on June 25. (Source: @shin_persian/7223)
Three blasts were reported in Sirik, Hormozgan Province, Iran, as a result of the CENTCOM strikes. (Source: @shin_persian/7221)

The IRGC issued a statement asserting that the CENTCOM strikes on Sirik will not go unanswered and a swift decisive response will be delivered at a time and place of their choosing. (Source: @shin_persian/7227)

Iran launched two drones at Bahrain; one was intercepted from the ground and one fell without causing damage. Bahrain accused Iran of a drone attack targeting the island kingdom, though Iran has not acknowledged the attack. (Source: @New_security8200/202119, @shin_persian/7231)

JD Vance stated that Iran signed a ceasefire agreement and if they have disagreements about implementation they can pick up the phone, but violence will be met with violence. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93261)

An oil tanker transiting from Qatar to Fujairah port in the UAE was hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, causing damage to the vessel's bridge, with all crew reported safe. (Source: @New_security8200/202119, @Faytuks_Network/4423)

Iranian banks including Bank Melli and Bank Saderat experienced service disruptions attributed to a cyberattack on banking infrastructure, with Informatics Services Corporation confirming the disruption is linked to traces of a previous cyberattack on technical infrastructure and centralized banking systems. (Source: @VahidOnline/76710)

Israel and Lebanon signed a US-mediated framework agreement in Washington requiring IDF withdrawal from the yellow line areas in southern Lebanon conditional on Hezbollah disarmament, with a pilot program in two areas where the Lebanese army will assume security responsibility. (Source: @yediotnews25/131976)

Prime Minister Netanyahu held a press conference stating the Lebanese government showed great courage and this is a major blow to Iran and Hezbollah, and that Israel will remain in the security zone. (Source: @amitsegal/62561, @almogboker78/17610)

IDF and Shin Bet confirmed the killing of Walid al-Nehé, deputy commander of the Nukhba company who commanded the October 7 infiltration of a terrorist cell into Israeli territory, in Gaza. (Source: @almogboker78/17608, @yediotnews25/131979)

National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir declared the Lebanon agreement a great mistake, stating Lebanon will not disarm Hezbollah, Hezbollah ministers sit in the Lebanese government, and only IDF soldiers will destroy Hezbollah. He demanded a cabinet vote. (Source: @pkpoi/143000)

An alarm was activated overnight over fears of militant infiltration in the settlement of Beit Arie Tzofar in the Benjamin Division of Samaria, following identification of two suspects; extensive security force sweeps found no suspects and the incident was declared ended. (Source: @New_security8200/202109, @New_security8200/202111, @tzevaadom_en/68508)

Defense Minister Katz instructed the IDF to prepare for a prolonged stay in the security zone and to prepare accordingly for the defense of IDF soldiers and removal of threats on northern communities. (Source: @yediotnews25/131976)

IDF and Shin Bet confirmed the killing of Walid al-Nehé, deputy commander of the Nukhba company who commanded the October 7 infiltration of a terrorist cell into Israeli territory, in Gaza. (Source: @almogboker78/17608, @N12chat/214854)

An alarm was activated overnight over fears of militant infiltration in the settlement of Beit Arie Tzofar in the Benjamin Division of Samaria, following identification of two suspects in the settlement; extensive security force sweeps found no suspects and the incident was declared ended. (Source: @New_security8200/202109, @New_security8200/202111, @tzevaadom_en/68508)

Israel and Lebanon signed a US-mediated framework agreement in Washington requiring IDF withdrawal from southern Lebanon conditional on Hezbollah disarmament, with a pilot in two areas where the Lebanese army will assume security responsibility. (Source: @yediotnews25/131976)

Hezbollah deputy secretary-general Naim Qassem declared the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement void and demanded it be replaced with the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, calling it a terrible mistake. (Source: @pkpoi/142995)
Hezbollah supporters demonstrated against the agreement with Israel on the road near Rafic Hariri Airport in Beirut. (Source: @lelotsenzura/93255)

Israeli drone strike targeted Al-Nabatiya Al-Fawqa in southern Lebanon, with reports of at least 4 drone strikes in the south during the day, injuring at least one Lebanese civilian as ceasefire violations persist. (Source: @presstv/195884, @redlinkleb/28660, @yediotnews25/131976)

Parliament Speaker Nabih Berri cautioned the Lebanese nation against being drawn into sedition following the US-sponsored framework agreement between the Beirut government and the Tel Aviv regime. (Source: @presstv/195883)
Hostile military aviation was reported flying in southern Lebanon airspace. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373176)
The Islamic Republic of Iran announced its solidarity with Iraq. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373186)

Iran launched two drones at Bahrain; one was intercepted from the ground and one fell without causing damage. Bahrain accused Iran of a drone attack targeting the island kingdom, though Iran has not acknowledged the attack. (Source: @New_security8200/202119, @shin_persian/7231)

An oil tanker transiting from Qatar to Fujairah port in the UAE was hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, causing damage to the vessel's bridge, with all crew reported safe and no environmental damage. (Source: @New_security8200/202119, @Faytuks_Network/4423)

sections.threat_assessment: The security environment remains highly volatile across multiple theaters. The CENTCOM strikes on Iranian military infrastructure in Hormozgan Province and Iran's retaliatory drone launch against Bahrain demonstrate active escalation between the US and Iran, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining a critical flashpoint following attacks on commercial shipping. In Lebanon, the framework agreement faces immediate rejection from Hezbollah leadership and continued Israeli drone strikes in the south, indicating the ceasefire is fragile and implementation uncertain. The IRGC's explicit threat of a swift and decisive response to the Sirik strikes signals further Iranian military action is imminent.
sections.regional_response: The US-brokered Israel-Lebanon framework agreement represents a significant diplomatic development, but faces strong opposition from Hezbollah's leadership which declared the agreement void and demanded its replacement with the US-Iran memorandum. Bahrain's accusation of an Iranian drone attack and the continued attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz indicate Gulf states remain directly threatened by Iranian military actions. Hezbollah supporters demonstrated in Beirut against the agreement, while Lebanese Parliament Speaker Berri cautioned against sedition, reflecting deep internal divisions within Lebanon over the deal.
sections.intelligence_notes: The gap between the signed Israel-Lebanon framework agreement and on-the-ground reality is significant, with Israeli drone strikes continuing in southern Lebanon and Hezbollah rejecting the deal outright. Iran's cyberattack on its own banking infrastructure suggests either internal instability or a broader pattern of cyber operations affecting critical infrastructure. The concentration of aerial refueling tankers in the region indicates sustained US military operations tempo in the CENTCOM area of responsibility. The killing of Walid al-Nehé, a senior Nukhba commander linked to October 7, represents a significant decapitation strike against Hamas's elite forces in Gaza.
